commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces a per-call punctuation array in `RepairContentBlockSeams` with a static `SearchValues<char>` and adds four fence-edge seam repair tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR is a performance cleanup with regression tests rather than a reported user-facing bug. Source inspection confirms the current fast path still allocates a new punctuation array before the PR change.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers should review or supply the required validation evidence; there is no narrow automated code repair to request.

target.title: [Repo Assist] perf+test: SearchValues(char) in RepairContentBlockSeams; fence-path edge-case tests
target.body:
🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

Two focused improvements to the streaming message pipeline:

### Task 8 — Performance: `SearchValues<char>` in `RepairContentBlockSeams`

**`src/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/Chat/OpenClawChatDataProvider.cs`**

`RepairContentBlockSeams` is called on every streaming assistant message chunk. Its fast-path guard contained:

```csharp
text.IndexOfAny(new[] { '.', '!', '?', ':' }) < 0
```

The `new[]` expression allocates a fresh `char[]` on **every call**, even when the method exits via the fast path (which is the common case for short or punctuation-free chunks). This is replaced with:

```csharp
private static readonly SearchValues<char> s_seamPunctChars = SearchValues.Create(".!?:");
...
text.AsSpan().IndexOfAny(s_seamPunctChars) < 0
```

`SearchValues<char>` (introduced in .NET 8, already used in `GatewayUrlHelper.cs` in this codebase) is:
- **Allocation-free at the call site** — the set is pre-built once at class load
- **SIMD-accelerated** — uses vectorised search instructions on eligible hardware

Also replaces `text.AsSpan(i).ToString()` with `text[i..]` — identical semantics, cleaner syntax.

### Task 9 — Testing: `ContentBlockSeamRepairTests.cs` edge cases

Added 4 tests that directly exercise the fence-splitting / range-indexer code path changed above:

| Test | What it covers |
|------|---------------|
| `FenceAtStartOfInput_NoProseBeforeFence` | `fenceStart == 0` → `Substring(0, 0)` returns `""` → no crash |
| `ProseAfterFence_UsesRangeIndexer` | Prose at offset > 0 after a closing fence — exercises `text[i..]` |
| `AdjacentFences_EmptyProseSegmentsBetweenFences` | Two adjacent fences with no prose between — no spurious `\n\n` injected |
| `SeamImmediatelyBeforeFence_RepairsProseAndPreservesFence` | Seam in prose that abuts a fence — seam repaired, fence verbatim |

*Note: `DeviceIdentityStore.ClearStoredTokens` was the original Task 9 target but PR #628 already covers that class with 6 dedicated tests — adding a duplicate file would conflict.*

## Test Status

- **`ContentBlockSeamRepairTests`**: **33 passed** (was 29, +4 new edge-case tests) ✅
- **`OpenClaw.Connection.Tests`**: **267 passed** (unchanged) ✅
- **`OpenClaw.Shared.Tests`**: 8 pre-existing failures on Linux (Windows path tests — verified baseline matches, unrelated to this change) ⚠️

## Trade-offs

- `SearchValues<char>` is a sealed internal type — no behavioural change, purely a runtime performance improvement.
- The 4 new tests are narrow (fence-path geometry) rather than comprehensive seam-content tests; the existing theory rows already cover the seam content space.
